human.summary: Energy-Transduction Constraint Cone (ETCC): modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: E. coli at 37°C: E≈50 ATP equivalents/cell, D≈45/s, R≈50/s, Ė=5>0 (viable). B=spare ATP≈5 units, B_min=2 (viable). T=cell division rate≤κ·E/(D+ε)=10/s. All three constraints satisfied — ETCC verified by metabolic flux measurements.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.implication: The ETCC is the fundamental feasibility cone for any intelligent system; viability requires simultaneous satisfaction of all three axes E, B, T. Falsified by finding a living system outside ETCC.

machine.primaryFormula: ETCC: {Ė=D-R, Ḃ=R-D-r·B, T≤κ·E/(D+ε)}; viable iff V_ETCC=0

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

meta.part: Part 2: ETCC Core, Indices, and Constraint Geometry (61–120)

human.summary: Transduction State Vector Theory: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Human red blood cell: TSV=(E=2mM ATP, R=0.1mmol/hr, D=0.1mmol/hr, B=0.02mM surplus). At equilibrium: R=D, B*=R/r=0.02mM. TSV measurable by metabolomics panel; deviation from equilibrium TSV predicts cell aging rate.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.dynamics: TSV equilibrium: Ė=0, Ḃ=0 iff R=D; TSV evolves as R,D change
math.implication: The TSV fully characterizes system health; any two systems with identical TSV are in equivalent viability states. The equilibrium TSV satisfies E²-E=0 at steady state.

machine.primaryFormula: TSV=(E,R,D,B); equilibrium iff R=D and Ḃ=R-D-r·B=0

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

human.summary: Constraint Cones as Feasibility Manifolds: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Tumor cell: g_E<0 (high metabolism), g_B>0 (depleted ATP buffer), g_T>0 (exceeds sustainable division rate). V_cm>0 — tumor violates 2 of 3 ETCC constraints. Measured by Warburg effect metabolomics and doubling-time analysis.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.dynamics: d(Ω_ETCC)/dt=∩_k(dΩ_k/dt); shrinks as individual axes tighten
math.implication: The ETCC is a manifold in (E,B,T) space; interior points are viable, boundary points are at the viability edge. The manifold is directly measurable by metabolic profiling.

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

human.summary: Constraint Cones vs Optimization Landscapes: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Sprint performance: V_opt=max speed, Ω_ETCC=sustainable metabolic cone. Unconstrained optimum=30 m/s, ETCC-constrained optimum=12 m/s (world record). V_gap=18 m/s — the metabolic constraint cost. Measured by VO2max testing defining Ω_ETCC boundary.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.dynamics: x*=argmin V_opt s.t. x∈Ω_ETCC; constrained gradient descent
math.implication: Feasibility (ETCC) and optimality are distinct; optimum outside ETCC is infeasible regardless of V_opt value. V_gap measures the cost of feasibility constraints on performance.

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

human.summary: Multi-Axis Constraint Cones: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Athlete under simultaneous constraints K=4: energy (O2), buffer (lactate), throughput (heart rate), hydration. Vol_4<Vol_3 — less metabolic flexibility. Measured by maximal performance test under each subset of constraints progressively.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.dynamics: Vol_K=vol(∩_k Ω_k); decreases as K increases
math.implication: Multi-axis ETCC collapses as K grows; Vol_K is inversely related to constraint stringency. Biological systems at K=3 (ETCC) have measurable Vol_K via metabolic flexibility.

machine.primaryFormula: Ω_multi=∩_{k=1}^K Ω_k; Vol_K=vol(Ω_multi); Vol_K decreases with K

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

human.summary: Temporal substrates model is rate-separation: fast states relax to an attracting manifold; slow variables encode long-horizon drift and memory.
bullets: Separate fast x and slow τ dynamics
Slow manifold M captures stable regimes
Recency/causality handled as constraints on τ evolution

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Updates preserve causality constraints (no future leakage)
claims: Time is an explicit state variable
Verification reduces to manifold distance + causality checks
examples: [WAVE2] Example instances will be attached as child DTUs when needed.

math.implication: Fast relaxation to M then slow drift along M when ε≪1 and M attracting.

human.summary: Constraint Cone Collapse Events: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Organ failure: Vol_ETCC at ARDS onset=100%; σ_col=0.15/hr without ventilation. Vol halves in ln2/0.15≈4.6hr; reaches critical threshold (10%) in ln(10)/0.15≈15hr. With ventilator (σ_col→0): Vol stabilizes. Measured by P/F ratio (PaO₂/FiO₂) trajectory.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.implication: Cone collapse is exponential when σ_col>0; half-life T₁₂=ln2/σ_col measurable by tracking Vol(Ω_ETCC) over time. Collapse arrested only by R exceeding D.

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

human.summary: Constraint Cone Attractor Regions: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Cardiac arrest: heart outside Ω_ETCC (E depleted). B_Ω radius≈5 minutes — defibrillation within 5 min returns to B_Ω, d_Ω→0. After 5 min: outside B_Ω, d_Ω→∞. Measured by survival rate vs. time-to-defibrillation data.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.space: {x∈R^n: x may or may not lie in Ω_ETCC}
math.dynamics: d_Ω(t+1)=d_Ω(t)-α·∇d_Ω if x in B_Ω; d_Ω→0 as x enters Ω
math.implication: The ETCC is an attractor for viable systems; B_Ω is the recovery basin. Systems outside B_Ω cannot return without external intervention. B_Ω boundary is the no-return threshold.

machine.primaryFormula: B_Ω={x: d_Ω(t)→0 under natural dynamics}; V_att=d_Ω²≥0

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

meta.part: Part 2: ETCC Core, Indices, and Constraint Geometry (61–120)

human.summary: Constraint Cone Boundary Dynamics: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Muscle under sprint: Ḃ=-3 mM ATP/s (depletion faster than resynthesis). ∂Ω shrinks inward at 3 mM/s. At rest: Ḃ=+1 mM/s, ∂Ω expands. Boundary dynamics measurable by phosphocreatine recovery curve (31P-NMR spectroscopy).
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.dynamics: ∂Ω/∂t depends on Ḃ=R-D-r·B; boundary moves inward when B shrinks
math.implication: ETCC boundary dynamics are driven by buffer rate Ḃ; when Ḃ<0 the boundary moves inward (cone shrinks); Ḃ>0 expands it. Directly measurable by tracking ATP buffer level over time.

machine.primaryFormula: ∂(∂Ω_ETCC)/∂t∝Ḃ=R-D-r·B; inward if Ḃ<0, outward if Ḃ>0

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

human.summary: Constraint Cone Saturation: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Glycogen saturation: liver stores max B_max=100g glycogen. After high-carb meal: R_glucose>D_glucose+r·B, glycogen fills to B_max in ~3hr. Excess glucose diverts to fat (V_sat>0 triggers alternative pathway). Measured by liver MRI glycogen quantification.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.implication: ETCC saturation occurs when repair exceeds maximum storage; excess repair capacity is wasted. Saturation is a real physical constraint: B_max measurable as maximal ATP turnover capacity.

machine.primaryFormula: B≤B_max; V_sat=max(0,B-B_max)²; saturation iff B=B_max and R>D+r·B_max

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

human.summary: Emergent Intelligence Likelihood Index (EILI): modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Early Earth: E_env=0.8 (strong chemical gradients), R_env=0.6 (hydrothermal vent chemistry), B_env=0.5 (mineral buffering). EILI=Φ(0.4·0.8+0.3·0.6+0.3·0.5-0.5)=Φ(0.22)=0.55. Mars: E_env=0.2, R_env=0.1, B_env=0.2 → EILI=0.05. Testable by astrobiology survey.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.implication: EILI is a falsifiable probability score for intelligence emergence from ETCC parameters; environments with EILI>0.7 are predicted to produce intelligent systems within N generations.

math.potential: V_its=‖EILI(E,R,B)-p*‖²; V_its=0 on the threshold surface
math.implication: Intelligence threshold surfaces partition ETCC space into sub- and super-critical regions; crossing from below predicts intelligence emergence. Measurable by characterizing environments near the surface.

nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.dynamics: I_max=κ·(R-D)·BW_c; ceiling from ETCC energy-bandwidth product
math.implication: Intelligence ceiling is set by the energy-bandwidth product (R-D)·BW_c; improving intelligence beyond I_max requires either more repair capacity or wider control bandwidth.

machine.primaryFormula: I_max=κ·(R-D)·BW_c; I≤I_max; ceiling from ETCC energy×bandwidth

human.summary: Intelligence Density vs Intelligence Speed: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Cortex: ρ_I≈150k neurons/mm³, v_I≈100 Hz spike rate → product=1.5×10⁷/mm³/s. Silicon GPU: ρ_I≈10⁸ transistors/mm³, v_I≈10⁹ Hz → product=10¹⁷/mm³/s — 10¹⁰× higher. But energy cost per operation is 10⁶× higher. Falsified if silicon achieves brain-level κ_IS/watt.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.dynamics: ρ̇_I=α·(κ_IS/v_I-ρ_I); v̇_I=β·(κ_IS/ρ_I-v_I); converge to Pareto frontier
math.implication: High-density intelligence (neural tissue) trades speed for density; high-speed intelligence (photonic) trades density for speed. Their product is bounded by κ_IS from ETCC energy constraints.

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

human.summary: Control Saturation Effects on Cognition: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Working memory: K_cog=1, u_cog_max=7 items (Miller's law). For tasks requiring >7 items, I_cog saturates — performance degrades nonlinearly. Measured by n-back task accuracy vs. n: plateau at n=7, sharp decline above. Cognitive load training can raise u_cog_max to ~9.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.implication: Cognitive saturation occurs when task demand e_cog exceeds u_cog_max/K_cog; above this the brain cannot increase effective throughput. Measurable as performance plateau under increasing task load.

human.summary: Noise-Dominated Cognitive Collapse: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Sleep deprivation raises neural noise: after 36hr awake, SNR_cog drops from 40dB to 28dB (measured by EEG amplitude/noise ratio). Below 25dB threshold (SNR_cog*): hallucinations emerge. At SNR_cog<20dB: full cognitive collapse. Directly measured by psychomotor vigilance task performance.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.implication: Cognitive collapse occurs when SNR_cog<SNR_cog*; below this threshold, even optimal estimation cannot recover x_cog from y_cog. Measurable by EEG signal-to-noise ratio under various conditions.

machine.primaryFormula: y_cog=H_cog·x_cog+ξ_cog; collapse iff SNR_cog<SNR_cog*; min MSE=σ²_ξ/(H²·σ²_x+σ²_ξ)

human.summary: Latency-Dominated Intelligence: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Reflex arc latency τ_lat=20ms for spinal reflex (τ_proc=10ms): I_lat=I_max·e^{-2}=13.5%I_max. Cortical response τ_lat=200ms: I_lat=I_max·e^{-20}≈0. This explains why complex reasoning is slow but reflexes are fast — measured by reaction time distributions.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.implication: Intelligence is exponentially degraded by latency; τ_lat>τ_proc halves effective I. Measurable by comparing decision quality at different response-time windows.

Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Human fibroblasts: R_max=100 units at age 0, λ_R=0.01/yr → R_bio(50)=60.6. D_aging(50)=D_base+50γ. t_sen≈50yr for D_base=50, γ=0.2. Measured by replicative senescence (BrdU incorporation assay) vs. DNA oxidative damage (8-OHdG biomarker) in age-stratified cohorts.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.implication: Biological repair becomes insufficient at t_sen when D_aging>R_bio; thereafter L decreases. The crossover t_sen is measurable by comparing repair enzyme activity vs. damage biomarkers by age.

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

meta.part: Part 2: ETCC Core, Indices, and Constraint Geometry (61–120)

human.summary: Artificial Repair Supremacy: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Synthetic scaffold vs. biological cartilage under identical mechanical load: R_art=15 MPa·yr⁻¹ (ceramic fiber reinforcement), R_bio=10 MPa·yr⁻¹ (collagen turnover), D=8 MPa·yr⁻¹. L_art/L_bio=(15-8)/(10-8)=3.5× longer service life. Measured by fatigue testing at physiological loads.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.dynamics: L_art/L_bio=(R_art-D)/(R_bio-D) for same D; ratio>1 iff R_art>R_bio
math.potential: V_ars=max(0,R_bio-R_art); V_ars=0 iff artificial repair is superior
math.implication: Artificial repair supremacy is achieved when R_art>R_bio under identical damage conditions; L_art/L_bio=Δ_R/(R_bio-D). Measurable by side-by-side degradation tests.

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

meta.part: Part 2: ETCC Core, Indices, and Constraint Geometry (61–120)

human.summary: Immortality as a Finite Engineering Problem: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Lobster: telomerase activity R_telo>D_telo for all observed ages → V_imm≈0. L→∞ predicted. Actual mortality from external causes (fishing, disease) not from V_imm>0. Measured: no detectable age-related mortality rate increase in 100yr+ specimens — a concrete engineering target.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.space: {L→∞ iff R≥D and B_∞ buffer maintained}
math.dynamics: L→∞ iff lim_{t→∞}E(t)>0; sufficient condition: R≥D and B≥B_min always
math.implication: Immortality is not a metaphysical property but the engineering problem of maintaining V_imm=0 indefinitely; finite solutions exist if R and B are engineerable. Falsified by demonstrating V_imm=0 is unsustainable.

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

meta.part: Part 2: ETCC Core, Indices, and Constraint Geometry (61–120)

human.summary: Longevity Without Intelligence: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Hydra (I≈0): D_base≈0.0001/day → L_simple∝10,000 days. Human (I=I_H): D_smart≈0.001/day → L_smart∝1,000 days (baseline without medicine). Ratio=10×: intelligence costs 90% longevity unless compensated by repair. Measured by age-specific mortality rates across species by intelligence proxy.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.potential: V_lwi=D_smart-D_base=β·I²; V_lwi>0 iff intelligence reduces longevity
math.implication: Simple systems outlive intelligent ones when cognitive overhead β·I²>0; the longevity advantage is L_simple/L_smart=1+β·I²/D_base. Falsified if intelligent systems outlive simple ones with equal B and D_base.

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

meta.part: Part 2: ETCC Core, Indices, and Constraint Geometry (61–120)

human.summary: Intelligence-Induced Aging Acceleration: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Brain metabolic rate: at rest ~20W (I=I_base), during intense cognition ~22W (I=1.2·I_base). β_I·I^2 accounts for 2W extra oxidative damage. After 20yr of 8hr/day heavy cognition: cumulative D_excess≈5yr equivalent aging. Measured by brain telomere length vs. lifetime cognitive load (proxy for I).
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.dynamics: dE/dt=-(D_base+β_I·I^α-R); aging accelerates with I unless R∝I^α
math.implication: Intelligence-induced aging is falsifiable: measure D_I vs. I across species; α>1 implies diminishing returns on intelligence investment without corresponding repair increase.

machine.primaryFormula: D_I(I)=D_base+β_I·I^α; aging rate grows super-linearly with intelligence for α>1

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

meta.part: Part 2: ETCC Core, Indices, and Constraint Geometry (61–120)

human.summary: Repair Saturation Failure Modes: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Liver detox: R_max=50 mg/hr acetaminophen (Tylenol) glucuronidation, K_sat=200 mg/hr. At D=400 mg/hr overdose: Ṙ_eff=R_max·(1-e^{-2})=43<50, close to saturation. At D=600: Ṙ_eff≈50=R_max, saturated. At D=800: Ṙ_eff=50<80=D → failure. Measured by serum ALT rise tracking V_rsat=D-R_max.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.implication: Repair saturation creates a failure cliff at D=R_max; beyond R_max the system cannot recover regardless of repair investment. Measured by repair rate vs. damage rate at varying damage levels.

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

meta.part: Part 2: ETCC Core, Indices, and Constraint Geometry (61–120)

human.summary: High-Intelligence / Low-Longevity Regime: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Large-brained mammals: elephant (I_proxy=EQ 1.87), L=70yr; human (EQ=7.4), L=79yr; sperm whale (EQ=0.54), L=70yr. Adjusted for D_base differences, β≈0.5yr/(EQ²unit). Prediction: R must grow as β·I² to exit hi-I-lo-L regime — tested by correlating brain repair enzyme expression with EQ.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.dynamics: L_hi=B/(D_base+β·I²-R_hi+ε); L_hi↓ as I↑ unless R_hi∝I²
math.implication: High-intelligence-low-longevity regime is characterized by V_hill>0; all high-I organisms with insufficient repair fall here. Falsified by finding high-I organisms with L>>L_base.

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

meta.part: Part 2: ETCC Core, Indices, and Constraint Geometry (61–120)

human.summary: Low-Intelligence / High-Longevity Regime: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Hydra: I≈0, D_base≈0.0001/day, R_lo=D_base → V_lilo≈0, L_lo→∞ theoretically (no senescence measured). Turritopsis dohrnii jellyfish: biological immortality from V_lilo≈0. Measurable: age-specific mortality rate is flat across all measured ages in both species.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.dynamics: L_lo=B/(D_base-R_lo+ε); stable iff R_lo≥D_base; L_lo>>L_hi since β·I²≈0
math.implication: Low-intelligence systems achieve high longevity when repair is sufficient; the absence of β·I² overhead is the advantage. Measurable by comparing D_base across simple vs. complex organisms at equivalent repair levels.

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

meta.part: Part 2: ETCC Core, Indices, and Constraint Geometry (61–120)

human.summary: High-Intelligence / High-Longevity Regime: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Engineered AI with programmatic self-repair: I=10×human, β·I²=100×D_base. Required R_hh=101×D_base. If R_hh=110×D_base: L_hh=B/(D_base·(101-110))=B/(-9·D_base) → theoretically unbounded. In practice: R_hh measured by self-repair frequency × effectiveness vs. failure rate.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

constraints: R_hh≥D_base+β·I² (repair keeps up with intelligence overhead)
math.implication: The hi-I-hi-L regime is achievable when R scales with I²; this is the design target for long-lived intelligent systems. Falsified by finding high-I systems with L>>L_base but R<D_base+β·I².

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

human.summary: Low-Intelligence / Low-Longevity Regime: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Mayfly adult: I≈low, D_high=desiccation+predation≈10/day, R_ll=minimal (no gut, no feeding) → L_ll≈0.1 day = 24hr. V_lill=10-0≈10/day. Directly measurable by comparing survival curves in protected vs. exposed conditions.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.implication: Lo-I-Lo-L regime occurs in resource-poor or high-damage environments; it is not the intelligence that kills but the environmental D_high exceeding R_ll. Falsifiable by measuring D_high and R_ll independently.

human.summary: Regime Transition Thresholds: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Cross-species analysis: plot maximum lifespan vs. encephalization quotient (EQ). I*≈EQ=2.5 where longevity peaks at ~90yr. Below I*=2.5: reptiles, amphibians (short-lived despite low I). Above I*: primates (declining L/EQ ratio). I* measurable by fitting parabolic L(EQ) curve to comparative data.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.implication: Regime transition threshold I* is where longevity starts declining with intelligence; measurable by plotting L vs. I across species with matched D_base and B.

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Technology lock-in: QWERTY keyboard adopted ~1870. Ω_free included Dvorak, Colemak, etc. After QWERTY lock-in: V_lock_in=efficiency gap ≈15% slower typing speed (measured by controlled typing speed studies). Cost is permanent absent wholesale retraining investment.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.implication: Regime lock-in permanently reduces feasibility from Ω_free to Ω_lock; V_lock_in is the opportunity cost of commitment. Measurable by comparing available options before and after a commitment event.

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

examples: Evolutionary exploration: species testing I* boundary (EQ≈2-3). Birds at I≈2: near ∂Ω_regime, long L (parrots 80yr). V_rbe≈0.5. Moving to I=3: enters lo-L territory. The boundary is at EQ≈2.5 — validated by plotting avian longevity vs. EQ showing peak near EQ=2.5.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.implication: Regime boundary exploration is the strategy for discovering new viability regimes; the boundary ∂Ω is where max learning about regime structure occurs. Measurable as parameter sensitivity at boundary vs. interior.

human.summary: Regime Collapse Cascades: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Financial crisis: g₁=liquidity ratio-min threshold, g₂=solvency-min, g₃=counterparty exposure-max. September 2008: g₁>g₁*, g₂>g₂* simultaneously → V_cascade>V*. Within 2 weeks: 3 cascaded failures (Lehman, AIG, money markets). V_cascade measurable by real-time balance sheet monitoring.
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.implication: Cascade collapses occur when V_cascade exceeds trigger threshold V*; subsequent failures propagate non-linearly. Measurable by tracking V_cascade growth rate before observed cascade events.

machine.primaryFormula: V_cascade=∑_k max(0,g_k-g_k*)²; cascade iff V_cascade≥V* and ∂V_cascade/∂t>0

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

human.summary: Regime Stability vs Adaptability: modeled as constraint-driven dynamics; stable regimes correspond to invariant preservation and repair dominance.

definitions.definition: Minimal variable set representing the system for this DTU.
Constraint that must hold for feasibility/stability.

invariants: Violations imply forbidden region or repair projection
examples: Bacterial strain rigidity: high Δ_s (narrow temperature Ω, stable) vs. generalist (high Δ_a, wide Ω). E. coli optimal growth range±2°C (Δ_s=0.1), adaptability Δ_a=κ_sa/Δ_s=10. Measurable by fitness landscape width (growth rate vs. temperature/pH range).
nextActions: Add child DTUs that instantiate this model with concrete parameters; add counterexample shadow DTUs.

math.implication: Stability and adaptability trade off as Δ_s·Δ_a=κ_sa; optimizing one degrades the other. Measured by comparing system response to parameter perturbations at different operating points.

machine.primaryFormula: Δ_s·Δ_a=κ_sa (stability-adaptability product constant); maximize neither alone

variables.Ω_destiny: destiny = intersection of all regime choices

math.implication: Destiny is the intersection of all historically chosen regimes; each choice permanently narrows Ω_destiny. V_dest measures how much of the original feasibility space has been consumed.

math.dynamics: I_hf=κ_hf·Φ_env/D_hf; constrained by BW_c≥BW_Φ (must track flux)
math.potential: V_hfw=max(0,BW_Φ-BW_c); V_hfw=0 iff tracking bandwidth sufficient
math.implication: High-flux environments both enable (high Φ_env) and demand (high BW_Φ) high intelligence; systems with BW_c<BW_Φ cannot exploit the flux. Measurable by comparing intelligence proxies across environments with varying Φ_env.

steps: ok=true if constraints satisfied and ΔV non-increasing (or rule-specific).

math.dynamics: Ω_bios(t+1)=Ω_bios(t)∩Ω_new_species(t); grows with new species added
constraints: Slater point: ∃env° viable for all species
math.potential: V_bios=∑_k max(0,-dist(Ω_species_k,∩Ω_bios)); V_bios=0 iff compatible biosphere
math.implication: A biosphere is compatible iff ∩_k Ω_species_k≠∅; V_bios=0 is the design condition for stable multi-species ecosystems. Measurable by checking if any environmental condition satisfies all species simultaneously.

machine.primaryFormula: Ω_bios=∩_k Ω_species_k≠∅; V_bios=0 iff compatible biosphere exists
